the diagram models a system with classes representing users, rankings, tips, and missions.
users can have rankings and complete missions.
the relationships between user and ranking/mission are one-to-many.
the diagram focuses on the data structure and relationships between these entities, without detailing any specific processes or interactions.

the diagram models a login sequence where the main component interacts with the firebase authentication service.
the `signinwithemailandpassword` method is called with email and password as arguments.
the flow proceeds only if the response from firebase authentication is not an error.
upon successful authentication, a token is created to identify the device.
the diagram focuses on the core authentication flow and doesn't show error handling beyond the initial response check.

the diagram models a user registration process.
the process begins with validation of email and password.
if validation passes, a user is created via firebase authentication.
the user data is then stored in a database.
a successful registration results in a firebaseuser being logged in.
error handling is present through the response != error checks.

realtime database -> main — type=message; label="risposta asincrona"; explanation="sends an asynchronous response back to the main application"
flow 1: main -> realtime database (conversione da hashmap a json, viene mandata una rest/http/2 request) -> realtime database -> main (risposta asincrona)
the diagram depicts an asynchronous communication flow between a main component and a realtime database component.
the main component sends a request to the realtime database after converting a hashmap to json.
the realtime database processes the request and sends an asynchronous response back to the main component.
the diagram focuses on the data update process, specifically the interaction for completing a rank or mission.

firebase sdk -> main — type=message; label="ondatachange()"; explanation="sends the data change notification to the main application"
flow 1: main -> firebase sdk (addvalueeventlistener(mission*)) -> realtime database (addvalueeventlistener(mission*)) -> firebase sdk (manda la risposta con i dati richiesti) -> main (ondatachange())
the diagram illustrates the data retrieval process from a realtime database using the firebase sdk.
the main component initiates the data request by sending a message to the firebase sdk.
the firebase sdk then forwards the request to the realtime database.
the realtime database responds with the requested data, which is then relayed back to the main component via the firebase sdk.
the `ondatachange()` message indicates a callback mechanism for handling data updates.
